114816.explanation: PRO Gulpen-Wittem aims for diversity in all layers of society, so the executive board must seriously seek representation that reflects society. Only if this is not possible, will more women than men—or vice versa—take positions.

114817.explanation: PRO values residents' participation and therefore believes that an advisory referendum should be made possible.

114820.explanation: Tourist pressure is already high for our residents and visitors. Additional farm campsites must therefore fit into the environment and are only possible under certain conditions.

114821.explanation: Distribution centers do not belong in our five-star landscape.

114823.explanation: Our housing stock is too small; PRO therefore advocates clear rules on housing. Housing seasonal workers on their own property can be part of this under conditions.

114831.explanation: That does not fit into our vulnerable landscape.

114835.explanation: PRO regrets that it has been abolished.

114838.explanation: A library is an essential basic facility. It is therefore good that the municipality contributes to the library in Gulpen. But we also applaud volunteer initiatives such as those in Epen and Wijlre and mini-libraries in the villages.

114839.explanation: Gulpen-Wittem needs a problem-oriented approach. PRO is therefore pleased with the hiring of street coaches following vandalism around the bus station and tackling (drug) crime in the rural areas. More attention is also needed for noise nuisance from road users.

114841.explanation: This is already arranged regionally and is adequate at the moment.

114845.explanation: There are still enough empty spots in the villages and much real estate suitable for renovation or rebuilding.

114847.explanation: PRO does not want vulnerable residents to sink further into poverty.

114848.explanation: People in poverty need to be helped to get out of it.

114850.explanation: Sustainability is very important to PRO.

114851.explanation: This is already possible in Gulpen-Wittem in villages that request it. On PRO's proposal, this is part of the mobility plan.

114852.explanation: PRO aims for diversity, so all healthcare providers are welcome everywhere.

114856.explanation: PRO is pleased with the agreement that we will only index from now on.

114857.explanation: There is no need for industrial areas in Gulpen-Wittem.

114859.explanation: PRO strives for a fair distribution. Therefore, a careful consideration is necessary instead of a rigid rule.

114861.explanation: "We believe the municipality should not prohibit solar panels on agricultural land. Small solar fields that are well integrated into the landscape can contribute to a successful energy transition."

114862.explanation: PRO is glad that Mosaqua has remained open and we continue to support this.

114865.explanation: PRO values the dialect, but schools determine their own curriculum.

114870.explanation: Fortunately we agreed this way in the accommodation policy.

114872.explanation: PRO prefers to promote the use of bicycles, public transport, car sharing, and parking spaces at the edges of villages.

114874.explanation: The permitting process needs improvement, but it is good that conditions can be imposed.

114875.explanation: PRO values quality over quantity. The pressure on our vulnerable landscape is already great with all the holiday parks and campsites.

114876.explanation: There is demand for this in some villages, but not in others. PRO wants to build according to need, with regard for the fragile countryside.

114816.explanation: Finding a suitable candidate should take priority over gender. If four women are better suited, then no man is appointed.

114817.explanation: In a referendum, emotions and short-term sentiments play too big a role to make sound decisions. For complex issues, it is impossible to convey the necessary knowledge. Furthermore, residents elect council members every four years to make decisions on their behalf.

114820.explanation: There is a big difference between a shop and a campsite. But the principle should be that entrepreneurs must be supported in their plans. This certainly applies to farmers.

114821.explanation: There is no place for this in our municipality. Nor is it under consideration.

114823.explanation: Mandating is not something we support.

114831.explanation: If it can be integrated into the landscape, we are certainly in favor. A beautiful way to tackle the housing shortage.

114835.explanation: The subsidies received by the clubs are sufficient to also involve youth. Music education is already provided in schools.

114838.explanation: A library is also a meeting and learning place. As long as the costs are manageable, multiple libraries can exist in the municipality.

114839.explanation: More police presence on the streets and more greenery in the landscape should create a greater feeling of safety and thereby reduce nuisance. Enforcement should be proactive but lenient.

114841.explanation: This depends on the demand for it. Perhaps a solution to tackle this together with neighboring municipalities.

114845.explanation: Also adding that street at the edges of the villages ensures that starters, families, and elderly can stay in their own village and that new residents can settle in a village.

114847.explanation: Where the capacity absolutely does not allow it due to force majeure, the municipality must remain lenient. However, this must not become a right.

114848.explanation: The municipality must adhere to the nationally imposed rules.

114851.explanation: Where possible, without burdening through roads, 30 km/h in the villages is sufficient.

114852.explanation: It's not about choosing, it’s about who has to pay. Everyone may choose the care they want, but the municipality pays for the nearest care.

114856.explanation: Forcing an increase in the tourist tax is not a good idea. Consideration should be given to another tourist tax system where, for example, day tourists also pay part of the tax.

114857.explanation: There is no space within our municipality.

114859.explanation: Our own residents must be given priority.

114861.explanation: There is no room for solar parks in our landscape.

114862.explanation: A swimming facility is a social and sporting amenity. This simply costs money. In the current situation, the costs are affordable and responsible.

114865.explanation: Limburgish should be a compulsory subject in primary schools. Especially in the context of the provincial goal to have Limburgish receive a European Level III recognition by 2030.

114870.explanation: We call those community centers, and these are essential within the villages.

114872.explanation: Where possible, parking spaces outside the centers, and public transport to the centers. In the centers, parking as much as possible for residents by permit, and paid parking for people from outside the municipality.

114874.explanation: Even more important is that the municipality works together with the organization towards a well-organized event.

114875.explanation: There are sufficient holiday parks and large campgrounds. This does not apply to small-scale camping.

114876.explanation: Adding that street is a good plan.

114816.explanation: This allocation depends on the candidates and competencies.

114817.explanation: Increases involvement and transparency. But with the right conditions.

114820.explanation: This must always be in consultation with the municipality, to prevent uncontrolled growth.

114821.explanation: That does not fit in the landscape of Gulpen-Wittem anyway.

114823.explanation: Employers must provide housing that at least meets legal obligations and is human-scale.

114831.explanation: Allowed on own property, but not on agricultural land.

114835.explanation: Available through the Youth Fund for Sport and Culture.

114838.explanation: A large library can offer more than small ones. Think of language lessons, computer courses, Digital Government Information.

114841.explanation: Within Gulpen-Wittem it is not mandatory. This can be addressed within the Lijn-50 (Vaals, Gulpen-Wittem, and Eijsden-Margraten) cooperation.

114845.explanation: Provincial policy is to add streets where possible. We support this.

114847.explanation: The automatic continuation must be stopped. Every year a new decision must be made whether to apply this or not. This must be tailored.

114848.explanation: We find the current policy sufficient.

114850.explanation: For a municipality of our size, we have sufficient charging points. Enforcement here is important, in our opinion.

114851.explanation: This depends on the core classification and the flow-through.

114852.explanation: Not feasible within the tightness of care; look at where possible to comply, but where not possible, safety and care take precedence over faith.

114856.explanation: Remains dependent on developments within the sector. It is also important to take into account the livability within the centers.

114857.explanation: Does not fit in our 5-star landscape.

114859.explanation: Given the small number of rental homes, that is not realistic.

114861.explanation: Must not come at the expense of our landscape.

114862.explanation: Enforce what has been agreed and recorded, but no additional funds.

114865.explanation: Our dialect is part of our cultural heritage. We must cherish it.

114870.explanation: Dependent on sufficient use and initiatives within the communities.

114872.explanation: This comes at the expense of the 5-star landscape and the green areas within the communities.

114874.explanation: This is only possible for the requirements for which the municipality itself is responsible.

114875.explanation: In our opinion, there are already enough large holiday parks and campsites.

114876.explanation: Where possibilities exist, definitely apply them. Especially if we endorse the Provincial policy of 'one more street'.

114816.explanation: Gender does not matter much, as long as there are qualified administrators with a professional attitude in the council.

114817.explanation: We have proposed a referendum ourselves. This proposal is currently being developed. We hope and expect that the council will implement the referendum during 2026.

114820.explanation: Farmers who want to transform their business should have the opportunity to explore new income models. It depends on the situation, for example whether there is already (too much) tourism. Competition with the local retail sector must also be considered.

114821.explanation: In our 5-star landscape, there is no place for distribution centers; due to the location of South Limburg relative to the rest of the Netherlands, this is also not logistically logical.

114823.explanation: If employers have to provide housing themselves, this can pose a risk to the quality of housing for seasonal workers. If it does happen, quality control is desired.

114831.explanation: If this applies to farmers, it should also apply to other residents. We consider unchecked growth in this area undesirable.

114835.explanation: Music is important for children’s personal development and making music together fosters social bonding.

114838.explanation: We are pleased with one central library in Gulpen-Wittem. Libraries are increasingly taking on functions for seniors and as meeting places, also in smaller communities. Therefore, we support the coexistence of both large and smaller libraries.

114839.explanation: Many of our residents sometimes feel unsafe. More municipal enforcement officers can increase the feeling of safety.

114841.explanation: Such homes should be located in a central place. Gulpen-Wittem is too small for such a facility.

114845.explanation: We support infill development within the communities and repurposing of vacant real estate. Accelerate the projects in Wijlre, the CPO Mechelen, and the Sophianum site. We are against 'one more street'.

114847.explanation: People with low incomes should be supported by waiving municipal taxes. This prevents problems from worsening and thereby avoids extra high costs for the parties involved.

114848.explanation: Inflation and rising energy costs are making life increasingly expensive. We want to protect low-income people from poverty because the consequences of financial worries are serious: debts, avoiding social contacts, poor sleep, unhealthy eating. By raising the income threshold, more people become eligible for municipal schemes.

114850.explanation: The steady increase of electric cars requires more charging stations.

114851.explanation: Among other things due to the increase of tourist traffic, many residents experience traffic nuisance and improper driving behavior. To make our villages more livable and safer, we aim for 30 km zones in villages and near schools. Additionally, we strive for safe school routes and bicycle streets in rural areas.

114852.explanation: There are already major staff shortages in healthcare, making the problem worse. Moreover, matching demand and supply of care in a small municipality is already difficult. Not feasible in a small municipality.

114856.explanation: User-pays principle: tourists benefit from municipal facilities and should contribute to them. The current level of the tourist tax is far from cost-covering.

114857.explanation: Depends on the type of activities and whether nature must be sacrificed (which we do not want) and if there are industrial estates nearby. In principle, we think concentrating business activities instead of dispersing them across the landscape is a good idea.

114859.explanation: Allocating scarce resources, such as social rental homes, for example by lottery, with chances of success depending on the severity of the previous living situation. The municipality can thus implement the Distribution Act without causing residents to have no chance.

114861.explanation: Priority: solar panels on roofs and parking lots. Installing them on agricultural land leads to landscape pollution, whereas the landscape is our most important asset. Solar panels damage our scenic landscape (small plots, hedgerows, and hedges). Reflective panels do not fit the natural appearance of the countryside.

114862.explanation: Swimming lessons at Mosaqua are vital for the residents of GW. Moreover, Mosaqua has a social function. Additionally, there is a positive effect on the local economy due to tourists who use Mosaqua.

114865.explanation: Regional language is an important part of our cultural heritage and traditions. Dialect is passed down from generation to generation but is under pressure due to aging population and migration from outside. Therefore, we stimulate it at primary schools.

114870.explanation: We find it important that every community has a meeting place, adapted to the size of the village. This does not necessarily have to be a building.

114872.explanation: Reduce cars in the center. Have tourist cars park on the edge of the center. Preserve the center for green spaces.

114874.explanation: It depends on the event. We cannot answer this question because it is unclear what is meant by a cultural event. Some events cause a lot of nuisance, others much less.

114875.explanation: We see that there is currently mass tourism in our municipality. This causes traffic congestion and nuisance. On the other hand, it brings in income. Mass tourism must give way to quality tourism. As far as we are concerned, there are already enough holiday parks and campsites in our municipality.

114876.explanation: 'One more street' damages our 5-star landscape. The housing problem can be solved by repurposing vacant real estate, splitting homes, mandatory owner-occupancy, banning conversion of homes into holiday homes, and taxing vacancies. Further progress in housing construction on Sophianum site, CPO Mechelen, and housing plans in Wijlre.

114816.explanation: We want the best people in government based on quality, without mandatory quotas. Diversity is welcome, but we select on merits, not gender.

114817.explanation: We value residents' involvement but prefer citizen participation and consultation over referenda. Public consultations can delay decision-making and do not suit effective governance.

114820.explanation: Enterprising farmers should have space to develop extra activities at their farm. This stimulates rural tourism and economic growth, with minimal barriers but with appropriate assessment.

114821.explanation: We are open to new companies, including distribution centers, provided they are located appropriately. A general ban unnecessarily hinders economic development and employment.

114823.explanation: It is reasonable that employers provide decent housing for their seasonal workers themselves. This reduces pressure on the regular housing market and keeps the living environment orderly.

114831.explanation: We encourage more housing construction, also in rural areas if sensible. Farmers with space should get the opportunity to build a few houses on their own land but with respect for the landscape and welfare regulations.

114835.explanation: Music education is valuable, but a general subsidy for all children is expensive and unfocused. We prefer to target support to children from less affluent families and let schools make their own choices in cultural education.

114838.explanation: One modern library can operate more efficiently and offer a wider range than fragmented small branches. By centralizing, we save costs and can still provide residents with good library services. We want to ensure accessibility for everyone.

114839.explanation: The VVD wants to invest in cooperation with neighboring municipalities; a larger and more flexible BOA pool makes our communities safer and more livable.

114841.explanation: Victims of domestic violence must have a safe shelter nearby. A 'Stay-away-from-me' house demonstrates that the municipality takes responsibility to protect vulnerable residents.

114845.explanation: We do not want to exclude new housing in advance when the villages are filling up. Although we cherish our 5-star landscape, limited expansion beyond the current built-up area should be possible to meet housing demand.

114847.explanation: We believe everyone should contribute according to their ability, but the very weakest must be spared. Completely stopping debt forgiveness would severely affect vulnerable residents; we want to maintain a limited safety net.

114848.explanation: Aid for poverty must remain targeted at those who truly need it. Raising the threshold pushes more people below the poverty line.

114850.explanation: We support electric driving, so the charging infrastructure must grow accordingly. By designating more charging stations, we make it easier for residents and visitors with electric cars, which is good for innovation and the environment.

114851.explanation: We support a 30 km/h speed limit on residential streets and near schools, but on main roads within the built-up area we want to maintain space for good traffic flow.

114852.explanation: Everyone must be able to choose a healthcare provider that suits their beliefs. Freedom of choice in healthcare is paramount; this increases trust and the quality of care provided.

114856.explanation: Our municipality thrives on tourism, so we must not deter visitors with higher costs. An attractive price for tourists keeps the sector healthy and competitive.

114857.explanation: If companies want to grow, the municipality must provide space for that. Responsible expansion of sites means new jobs and economic progress, which is good for our prosperity.

114859.explanation: Status holders need housing, but this must be balanced with and not come at the expense of residents who have been waiting a long time (in accordance with national/provincial regulations).

114861.explanation: Sustainable energy is important, but we disapprove of meadows full of solar panels in our 5-star landscape.

114862.explanation: The Mosaqua swimming pool is important for swimming lessons and recreation. We want to keep the pool open with smart financial management or regional cooperation, instead of abruptly stopping support.

114865.explanation: Learning the dialect (South Limburgish) at school is fun but not a core task of education. Schools should decide on this themselves; we prioritize basic skills and leave cultural education to the schools.

114870.explanation: Each community must have an accessible meeting place, but it doesn't always have to be a separate village center. We choose shared, multifunctional use of existing facilities and invest only where there is need, support, and a viable operation.

114872.explanation: Additional parking spaces improve access to shops and amenities. Where possible, the VVD wants to invest in smart parking facilities so that village centers remain accessible for everyone.

114874.explanation: We cut unnecessary rules to make organizing cultural events easier. Less bureaucracy means good initiatives proceed faster, benefiting the local cultural life.

114875.explanation: A total stop on new parks hampers quality projects and investments. We handle our space carefully, strive for a balance between tourism and livability, but do not exclude entrepreneurs in advance if they want to develop something small-scale and appropriate.

114876.explanation: To provide living space for young families and starters, we support small-scale new construction in each village. Additional homes keep villages vibrant and meet local needs without overburdening the environment.

statements.title: An extra street should be built at each village in the municipality.
The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: There should be one larger library in Gulpen-Wittem instead of smaller libraries in each village.
More municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety should be employed in Gulpen-Wittem.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The residents of Gulpen-Wittem should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Gulpen-Wittem moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should impose fewer requirements for permits for cultural events.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet minder eisen stellen voor een

titleParts.glossary: Permission from the municipality to do something.
A temporary, organized gathering. For example a street party, a flea market, or a music festival.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Business parks in Gulpen-Wittem should be allowed to expand.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to include the South Limburg dialect.
A women's shelter should be established in Gulpen-Wittem.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
There should be more parking spaces in the town centers.

titleParts.text: Er moeten meer parkeerplaatsen komen in de

titleParts.glossary: A village or small town that is part of the municipality.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: All villages in the municipality must have their own village center.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Gulpen-Wittem should stop providing funding to Zwembad Mosaque.
No new holiday parks or campsites should be added in the municipality.
